mechanism_narrative: A 5% YoY growth in real GDP signals a strengthening labor market and rising aggregate demand, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods of real GDP growth < 5% YoY are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 10% decline in the industrial production index signals a weakening supply chain and reduced capacity utilization, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods of industrial production decline < 10% are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 50-bp increase in the federal funds rate signals a tightening monetary cycle that compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods of 50-bp FOMC rate hikes are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5% increase in the unemployment rate signals a weakening labor market and reduced aggregate demand, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods of unemployment rate increase > 0.5% are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 10% YoY growth in real GDP and a 5% increase in the unemployment rate signal a strengthening labor market and rising aggregate demand, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods of real GDP growth < 10% YoY and unemployment rate increase > 5% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 20% decline in the industrial production index and a 10% increase in the unemployment rate signal a weakening supply chain and reduced capacity utilization, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods of industrial production decline < 20% and unemployment rate increase > 10% are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 25-bp increase in the federal funds rate signals a tightening monetary cycle that compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods of 25-bp FOMC rate hikes are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 15% YoY growth in real GDP and a 3% increase in the unemployment rate signal a strengthening labor market and rising aggregate demand, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods of real GDP growth < 15% YoY and unemployment rate increase > 3% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
